text.title: Spec Fields, Error States, and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Required fields for an AI assistant spec, fallback and error state rules, undo semantics, and agent failure modes.

section.title: Spec Required Fields

list.item: Trigger — event that opens the assistant (explicit user action required, not passive observation)
Context passed to AI — list of design context fields sent with the request
Max response wait — milliseconds before showing skeleton/timeout state
Fallback state — what to show if AI returns empty or error
Human confirmation — required before / after AI action (for destructive actions: always before)
Undo behavior — how AI actions integrate with the tool's undo/redo stack

section.reference: Per-feature AI assistant spec template with all required fields
Scored matrix for sidebar vs modal vs inline selection

rule.statement: Every AI assistant pattern must have an explicit fallback state for: AI returns no result, AI returns error, AI request times out.
rule.rationale: Missing fallback states are the most common omission in agent-generated specs; they produce blank UI or silent failures that users report as bugs.

rule.statement: Never use AI-generated copy for AI assistant error states. Human copywriters must own this text.
rule.rationale: AI errors inside AI assistants compound distrust; the error copy is the most trust-sensitive text in the product and must not be auto-generated.

rule.statement: Specify undo behavior explicitly for every AI action. AI actions must integrate with the tool's undo/redo stack as single atomic steps.
rule.rationale: Agents do not account for undo/redo semantics of AI actions in design tools; this is a consistent omission that causes user data loss (unexpected state after Cmd+Z).

antipattern.description: Agent specifies trigger condition that fires on every selection change.
antipattern.reason: Over-triggering reduces trust; enforce debounce and explicit user intent in all trigger specs produced by agents.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated copy for AI assistant UI uses phrases like "Ask me anything!" or "I can help with everything!".
antipattern.reason: These phrases set unrealistic expectations and increase user disappointment; human copywriters must review all AI assistant UI copy.

antipattern.description: Modal pattern spec omits focus trap and keyboard dismiss behavior.
antipattern.reason: Missing focus trap in modal patterns is an accessibility blocker (WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.2); agents consistently omit this.
